title: Airbnb's Hotel Strategy Is Now Much Bigger
text: Airbnb is expanding its hotel ambitions beyond initial pilots in highly regulated cities, moving to a more comprehensive approach that prioritizes independent and boutique hotels. CEO Brian Chesky highlighting the massive opportunity in hotels and plans to make hotel stays a larger part of the business. The company is repositioning hotels as a much larger opportunity that can support growth, improve supply reliability, and attract new categories of travelers. Airbnb is currently concentrating on onboarding smaller boutique and independent hotels rather than large hotel groups, as these properties align more closely with the unique, experience-driven positioning that originally defined the platform. The hotel pilots were concentrated in cities with strict short-term rental regulations, where expanding home-sharing inventory has become increasingly difficult, such as New York City, where Local Law 18 significantly reduced available short-term rental listings. Airbnb Luxe could re-emerge as a clearly defined premium category, featuring vetted inventory and optional add-ons such as enhanced guest screening and private services. The company has expanded its property types to include vacation homes, unique spaces, B&Bs, and boutique hotels, while also entering the luxury market through Beyond by Airbnb. In May 2025, Airbnb launched Airbnb Services, with offerings currently available in 260 cities allowing customers to book personalized services. The company is offering services integrated into the Airbnb app via the Trip Tab, consolidating travel planning into a single interface.
